source_intent.raw_intent: Case 011: I am a founder_operator in Canada. Build a research_agent_prompt for content_system using the operator_lens. Chaos input type is schema_blur. The source is intentionally messy: mix missing context, urgent language, partial constraints, and ambiguous acceptance criteria. Produce a governed artifact with explicit validation gates. Return something SR8 can compile into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract.

parsed_tester_json.compiled_intent: Objective: Design and deploy a research agent prompt for a content system that enables a founder-operator in Canada to generate, curate, and validate content assets using an operator lens, with explicit validation gates to ensure policy compliance and claim limits. Scope: - The research agent prompt must accept ambiguous, partial, and urgent input (schema_blur), resolve missing context via default heuristics, and produce structured research outputs that feed into content generation. The system must operate within Canadian content regulations and platform-specific claim limits. Constraints: - Must comply with Canadian content laws (e.g., CRTC guidelines, anti-spam legislation). - Must enforce claim limits per content asset (e.g., no unsubstantiated health, financial, or performance claims). - Must handle schema_blur input by inferring missing fields from context or fallback defaults. - Must include explicit validation gates at input ingestion, research synthesis, and output generation stages. - Must be SR8-compilable into objective, scope, constraints, success criteria, and output contract. Success Criteria: - Research agent prompt produces a structured research report with at least 3 sources per content topic. - Validation gates flag any claim exceeding predefined limits (e.g., 'cures disease' triggers block). - Schema_blur input is resolved without user intervention in >90% of test cases. - Output contract is machine-parseable and includes source_policy_and_claim_limits compliance evidence. Output Contract: - format: JSON - fields: research_summary: string (max 2000 chars); sources: array of objects (url, credibility_score, date); claim_audit: array of objects (claim_text, limit_status, policy_reference); validation_gates: array of objects (gate_name, passed, evidence); compliance_evidence: object (policy_version, claim_limits_applied, jurisdiction) - validation_rules: All claims must be within Canadian claim limits (e.g., no therapeutic claims without Health Canada approval).; Sources must have credibility_score >= 0.7.; Validation gates must all pass before output is released.
